@@ -771,7 +771,7 @@
 {"id":"bd-7ijsy","title":"xf: Implement --robot-xf-search command","description":"## Overview

Add `ntm --robot-xf-search` command for searching X/Twitter archives via xf integration.

## IMPORTANT: API Design Consistency

This command follows the **harmonized** robot mode patterns for tool bridges:
- **Naming**: `--robot-xf-search` (tool prefix: xf, no inline value)
- **Query param**: `--query=QUERY` (standard modifier flag)
- **Shared modifiers**: `--limit=N`, `--since=DATE`, `--until=DATE`, `--offset=N` (global flags)
- **Tool-specific modifiers**: `--xf-mode=`, `--xf-sort=` (unique to xf)

**NOTE**: This supersedes bd-1osrc which proposed a different interface. This bead is the canonical implementation.

## Command Interface

```bash
# Basic search (uses --query= modifier, NOT inline value)
ntm --robot-xf-search --query="error handling patterns"

# With search mode
ntm --robot-xf-search --query="rust async" --xf-mode=semantic --limit=10

# With date range filter
ntm --robot-xf-search --query="claude code" --since=2024-01-01 --until=2024-06-30

# With pagination
ntm --robot-xf-search --query="ai agents" --limit=20 --offset=20

# Sort by date (newest first)
ntm --robot-xf-search --query="llm" --xf-sort=date
```

## Output Schema

```json
{
  "success": true,
  "timestamp": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  "query": "error handling patterns",
  "mode": "hybrid",
  "sort": "relevance",
  "count": 5,
  "offset": 0,
  "hits": [
    {
      "type": "tweet",
      "date": "2023-06-15",
      "content": "Been thinking about error handling patterns in Rust...",
      "score": 0.95,
      "url": "https://x.com/user/status/123456",
      "author": "rustacean",
      "thread_id": "123456"
    }
  ],
  "total_matches": 150,
  "has_more": true,
  "_agent_hints": {
    "next_offset": 5,
    "pages_remaining": 29,
    "suggested_query_refinement": null
  }
}
```

## Error Response

```json
{
  "success": false,
  "timestamp": "2024-01-15T10:30:00Z",
  "error": "xf not found in PATH",
  "error_code": "TOOL_NOT_FOUND",
  "hint": "Install xf: go install github.com/.../xf@latest"
}
```

## Modifier Flags

| Flag | Description | Default | Scope |
|------|-------------|---------|-------|
| `--query=Q` | Search query (required) | - | **Required** |
| `--xf-mode=M` | Search mode: keyword, semantic, hybrid | hybrid | Tool-specific |
| `--xf-sort=S` | Sort order: relevance, date, date-asc | relevance | Tool-specific |
| `--limit=N` | Max results to return | 20 | Global shared |
| `--offset=N` | Skip first N results (pagination) | 0 | Global shared |
| `--since=DATE` | Filter to tweets after date (YYYY-MM-DD) | none | Global shared |
| `--until=DATE` | Filter to tweets before date (YYYY-MM-DD) | none | Global shared |

## Code Location
- internal/robot/xf_search.go

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] `--robot-xf-search` flag registered in root.go (bool flag)
- [ ] `--query=` required modifier for search term
- [ ] `--xf-mode=` accepts: keyword, semantic, hybrid (default: hybrid)
- [ ] `--xf-sort=` accepts: relevance, date, date-asc (default: relevance)
- [ ] `--limit=N` caps results (default 20) - uses global shared flag
- [ ] `--offset=N` skips first N results for pagination - uses global shared flag
- [ ] `--since=DATE` filters by start date - uses global shared flag
- [ ] `--until=DATE` filters by end date - uses global shared flag
- [ ] JSON output matches schema exactly (includes timestamp, offset, total_matches)
- [ ] Missing xf returns actionable error with hint and error_code
- [ ] Empty results returns success with empty hits array
- [ ] Exit code 0 on success, 1 on error, 2 if xf unavailable
- [ ] `_agent_hints` includes `next_offset` for pagination guidance

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests (internal/robot/xf_search_test.go)

| Test Case | Description |
|-----------|-------------|
| `TestXFSearchFlagParsing` | Parse --query, --xf-mode, --xf-sort, --limit, --offset, --since, --until |
| `TestXFSearchQueryRequired` | Error when --query is missing |
| `TestXFSearchModeValidation` | Accept keyword/semantic/hybrid, reject invalid modes |
| `TestXFSearchSortValidation` | Accept relevance/date/date-asc, reject invalid sorts |
| `TestXFSearchDateParsing` | Parse --since and --until dates in YYYY-MM-DD format |
| `TestXFSearchDateValidation` | Reject invalid date formats |
| `TestXFSearchDateRangeValidation` | Error if --until is before --since |
| `TestXFSearchOffsetValidation` | Accept non-negative integers, reject negative |
| `TestXFSearchOutputParsing` | Parse xf JSON output correctly |
| `TestXFSearchScoreNormalization` | Normalize scores to 0.0-1.0 range |
| `TestXFSearchJSONSerialization` | Output matches schema with all required fields |
| `TestXFSearchTimestampIncluded` | Response includes RFC3339 timestamp |
| `TestXFSearchMissingXF` | Error with TOOL_NOT_FOUND when xf not in PATH |
| `TestXFSearchXFFailure` | Handle xf command failures gracefully |
| `TestXFSearchEmptyResults` | Return success with empty hits array |
| `TestXFSearchExitCodes` | Map errors to correct exit codes (0/1/2) |
| `TestXFSearchLimitEnforced` | Limit caps results correctly |
| `TestXFSearchOffsetApplied` | Offset skips correct number of results |
| `TestXFSearchHasMoreFlag` | Set has_more when total_matches > offset + count |
| `TestXFSearchNextOffsetHint` | Calculate next_offset correctly in _agent_hints |
| `TestXFSearchPagesRemaining` | Calculate pages_remaining correctly |
| `TestXFSearchThreadIdIncluded` | Include thread_id in hit objects |

### E2E Tests (scripts/e2e_robot_xf.sh)

```bash
#!/bin/bash
# =============================================================================
# [E2E-XF] Robot XF Search End-to-End Tests
# =============================================================================
# Tests the --robot-xf-search command for X/Twitter archive integration.
# Exit codes: 0=all passed, 1=test failed, 77=skipped (xf not installed)
# =============================================================================

set -euo pipefail

LOG_PREFIX="[E2E-XF]"
PASSED=0
FAILED=0
SKIPPED=0

log_info()  { echo "$LOG_PREFIX [INFO]  $*"; }
log_pass()  { echo "$LOG_PREFIX [PASS]  $*"; ((PASSED++)); }
log_fail()  { echo "$LOG_PREFIX [FAIL]  $*"; ((FAILED++)); }
log_skip()  { echo "$LOG_PREFIX [SKIP]  $*"; ((SKIPPED++)); }
log_debug() { echo "$LOG_PREFIX [DEBUG] $*"; }

cleanup() {
    log_info "Test summary: $PASSED passed, $FAILED failed, $SKIPPED skipped"
}
trap cleanup EXIT

log_info "Starting robot xf E2E tests"
log_info "Test timestamp: $(date -Iseconds)"

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Prerequisite Check
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Checking prerequisites..."

if ! command -v xf &>/dev/null; then
    log_skip "xf not installed - skipping all tests"
    exit 77
fi

XF_VERSION=$(xf --version 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")
log_info "xf version: $XF_VERSION"

if ! command -v jq &>/dev/null; then
    log_fail "jq is required for tests"
    exit 1
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test XF-001: Basic search with --query
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test XF-001: Basic search with --query"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-xf-search --query="test query" 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.query == "test query"' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e 'has("timestamp") and has("total_matches") and has("offset")' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            log_pass "XF-001: Basic search works with all required fields"
        else
            log_fail "XF-001: Missing required fields (timestamp, total_matches, or offset)"
        fi
    else
        log_fail "XF-001: Query not echoed in response"
    fi
else
    log_fail "XF-001: Search did not succeed"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test XF-002: Search with --xf-mode=semantic
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test XF-002: Search with --xf-mode=semantic"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-xf-search --query="rust async" --xf-mode=semantic 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.mode == "semantic"' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log_pass "XF-002: Semantic mode accepted"
else
    log_fail "XF-002: Mode not set to semantic"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test XF-003: Search with --xf-mode=keyword
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test XF-003: Search with --xf-mode=keyword"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-xf-search --query="rust" --xf-mode=keyword 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.mode == "keyword"' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log_pass "XF-003: Keyword mode accepted"
else
    log_fail "XF-003: Mode not set to keyword"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test XF-004: Search with --limit
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test XF-004: Search with --limit=5"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-xf-search --query="programming" --limit=5 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

HITS_COUNT=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq '.hits | length' 2>/dev/null || echo "-1")
if [[ "$HITS_COUNT" -le 5 ]]; then
    log_pass "XF-004: Limit respected (got $HITS_COUNT hits)"
else
    log_fail "XF-004: Limit exceeded (expected <=5, got $HITS_COUNT)"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test XF-005: Search with --since date filter
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test XF-005: Search with --since=2024-01-01"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-xf-search --query="ai" --since=2024-01-01 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log_pass "XF-005: --since date filter accepted"
else
    log_fail "XF-005: Search with --since failed"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test XF-006: Search with --until date filter
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test XF-006: Search with --until=2024-12-31"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-xf-search --query="ai" --until=2024-12-31 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log_pass "XF-006: --until date filter accepted"
else
    log_fail "XF-006: Search with --until failed"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test XF-007: Search with date range (--since and --until)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test XF-007: Search with date range"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-xf-search --query="llm" --since=2024-01-01 --until=2024-06-30 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log_pass "XF-007: Date range accepted"
else
    log_fail "XF-007: Date range search failed"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test XF-008: Invalid --xf-mode value
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test XF-008: Invalid --xf-mode value"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-xf-search --query="test" --xf-mode=invalid 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == false' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.error_code' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        log_pass "XF-008: Invalid mode rejected with error_code"
    else
        log_fail "XF-008: Missing error_code in error response"
    fi
else
    log_fail "XF-008: Invalid mode should fail"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test XF-009: Empty results handling
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test XF-009: Empty results handling"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-xf-search --query="xyznonexistentquery98765" 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.hits == []' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.total_matches == 0' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            log_pass "XF-009: Empty results handled correctly"
        else
            log_pass "XF-009: Empty hits array (total_matches may differ)"
        fi
    else
        log_pass "XF-009: No results handled (may have unexpected matches)"
    fi
else
    log_fail "XF-009: Empty results should still succeed"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test XF-010: Missing --query parameter
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test XF-010: Missing --query parameter"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-xf-search 2>&1) || true
EXIT_CODE=$?
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"
log_debug "Exit code: $EXIT_CODE"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == false' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.error' 2>/dev/null | grep -qi "query.*required"; then
        log_pass "XF-010: Missing query correctly rejected"
    else
        log_fail "XF-010: Error message should mention query is required"
    fi
elif echo "$RESULT" | grep -qi "query.*required"; then
    log_pass "XF-010: Missing query correctly rejected (stderr)"
else
    log_fail "XF-010: Should require --query"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test XF-011: Default mode is hybrid
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test XF-011: Default mode is hybrid"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-xf-search --query="default mode test" 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.mode == "hybrid"' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log_pass "XF-011: Default mode is hybrid"
else
    MODE=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.mode' 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")
    log_fail "XF-011: Expected default mode hybrid, got $MODE"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test XF-012: Pagination with --offset
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test XF-012: Pagination with --offset"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-xf-search --query="code" --limit=5 --offset=10 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    OFFSET=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq '.offset' 2>/dev/null || echo "-1")
    if [[ "$OFFSET" -eq 10 ]]; then
        log_pass "XF-012: Offset applied correctly"
    else
        log_fail "XF-012: Offset not reflected in response (expected 10, got $OFFSET)"
    fi
else
    log_fail "XF-012: Pagination failed"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test XF-013: Sort by date
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test XF-013: Sort by date"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-xf-search --query="test" --xf-sort=date 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    SORT=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq -r '.sort' 2>/dev/null || echo "unknown")
    if [[ "$SORT" == "date" ]]; then
        log_pass "XF-013: Sort by date accepted"
    else
        log_fail "XF-013: Sort not set to date (got $SORT)"
    fi
else
    log_fail "XF-013: Sort by date failed"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test XF-014: Agent hints with pagination info
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test XF-014: Agent hints with pagination info"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-xf-search --query="programming" --limit=5 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e 'has("_agent_hints")' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
        if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '._agent_hints | has("next_offset")' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
            log_pass "XF-014: Agent hints include next_offset"
        else
            log_fail "XF-014: Agent hints missing next_offset"
        fi
    else
        log_fail "XF-014: Missing _agent_hints"
    fi
else
    log_fail "XF-014: Search failed"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test XF-015: Combined options
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test XF-015: Combined options"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-xf-search --query="rust" --xf-mode=semantic --xf-sort=date --limit=3 --offset=5 --since=2023-06-01 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == true and .mode == "semantic" and .sort == "date"' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    HITS_COUNT=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq '.hits | length' 2>/dev/null || echo "-1")
    OFFSET=$(echo "$RESULT" | jq '.offset' 2>/dev/null || echo "-1")
    if [[ "$HITS_COUNT" -le 3 ]] && [[ "$OFFSET" -eq 5 ]]; then
        log_pass "XF-015: Combined options work correctly"
    else
        log_fail "XF-015: Limit or offset not respected with combined options"
    fi
else
    log_fail "XF-015: Combined options failed"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test XF-016: Exit code verification
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test XF-016: Exit code verification"
ntm --robot-xf-search --query="test" --xf-mode=invalid >/dev/null 2>&1 || EXIT_CODE=$?
if [[ "${EXIT_CODE:-0}" -eq 1 ]]; then
    log_pass "XF-016: Exit code 1 for invalid input"
else
    log_fail "XF-016: Expected exit code 1, got ${EXIT_CODE:-0}"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Test XF-017: Invalid date range (until before since)
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "Test XF-017: Invalid date range"
RESULT=$(ntm --robot-xf-search --query="test" --since=2024-06-01 --until=2024-01-01 2>&1) || true
log_debug "Raw output: $RESULT"

if echo "$RESULT" | jq -e '.success == false' >/dev/null 2>&1; then
    log_pass "XF-017: Invalid date range rejected"
else
    log_fail "XF-017: Should reject --until before --since"
fi

# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
# Final Summary
# -----------------------------------------------------------------------------
log_info "========================================="
log_info "Final Results: $PASSED passed, $FAILED failed, $SKIPPED skipped"
log_info "========================================="

if [[ $FAILED -gt 0 ]]; then
    exit 1
fi

log_info "All robot xf E2E tests passed!"
exit 0
```

## API Harmonization Notes

This bead was updated to follow the canonical robot mode tool bridge pattern:
- Tool bridges use `--robot-<tool>-<action>` (bool flag)
- Parameters use modifier flags (`--query=`, `--limit=`)
- Global shared flags (`--limit`, `--since`, `--until`, `--offset`) are NOT tool-prefixed
- Tool-specific flags (`--xf-mode`, `--xf-sort`) ARE tool-prefixed

 {"id":"bd-7ny3","title":"EPIC: Progressive CLI Discovery System - Tiered Command Visibility","description":"## Overview
Implement a proficiency-tiered command discovery system that progressively reveals NTM commands as users gain experience. This reduces cognitive overload for new users while preserving full power for experts.

## Problem Statement
NTM has 40+ commands and flags. New users face a wall of options in --help, making the tool feel overwhelming. Meanwhile, power users need quick access to advanced features. Current flat --help listing serves neither audience well.

## Design Philosophy
- Tier 1 (Apprentice): Core workflow commands only (spawn, send, status, kill, help)
- Tier 2 (Journeyman): Full standard commands (all current public commands)
- Tier 3 (Master): Advanced/experimental features (robot mode, internal debugging)

## Key Features
1. Tiered help output: Commands filtered by proficiency level
2. Automatic tier promotion: Track usage patterns to suggest promotions
3. ntm level command: View/change proficiency tier
4. Contextual suggestions: Suggest relevant commands based on current state
5. Progressive disclosure in dashboard: Show only tier-appropriate controls

## Configuration
- Store tier in ~/.config/ntm/proficiency.json
- Support NTM_PROFICIENCY_TIER env var override
- Default new users to Tier 1

## Success Criteria
- New users see only 5-7 essential commands initially
- Help output adapts to proficiency tier
- Users can manually upgrade/downgrade tier
- Dashboard respects tier for keybinding hints
- Contextual suggestions appear for common next actions

## Files Likely Affected
- internal/cli/help.go (new file for tiered help)
- internal/cli/root.go (tier-aware command registration)
- internal/config/proficiency.go (new file for tier management)
- internal/tui/dashboard/help.go (tier-aware hints)

## Implementation Phases
1. Define tier taxonomy and command categorization
2. Implement proficiency config storage
3. Create tiered help renderer
4. Add usage tracking for auto-promotion
5. Implement contextual suggestions
6. -{"id":"bd-852e","title":"Task: Add --local and --ollama flags to spawn command","description":"## Overview
Extend the spawn command to support creating local LLM agent sessions.

## Deliverables
1. Update internal/cli/spawn.go:
   - Add --local=N flag: spawn N local agents
   - Add --ollama=N flag: alias for --local (explicit Ollama)
   - Add --local-model=<model> flag: specify model (default: codellama:latest)
   - Add --local-host=<url> flag: custom Ollama host

2. Flag combinations:
   - ntm spawn myproject --local=2 (2 local agents)
   - ntm spawn myproject --local=2 --local-model=deepseek-coder:33b
   - ntm spawn myproject --cc=2 --local=2 (mixed fleet)

3. Validation:
   - Check Ollama is running before spawn
   - Check model exists (offer to pull if not)
   - Validate model name format

4. Session naming:
   - Local agents: myproject__ollama_1, myproject__ollama_2
   - Or: myproject__local_1 for generic naming
   - Include model name in agent metadata

## Implementation Notes
- Integrate with existing spawn infrastructure
- Reuse pane management code
- Store local agent config in session metadata

## Acceptance Criteria
- --local flag spawns local agent sessions
- Model selection works
- Mixed fleets function correctly
- Helpful errors when Ollama unavailable

 {"id":"bd-8o977","title":"CAAM Integration: Coding Agent Account Manager for Rate Limit Recovery","description":"Integrate CAAM (coding_agent_account_manager) for instant account switching when agents hit rate limits.

## Project Location
/dp/coding_agent_account_manager (Rust CLI)

## Problem Being Solved

Rate limits are the #1 operational pain point in multi-agent workflows:
- Claude Code: 5-minute to multi-hour cooldowns
- OpenAI Codex: Per-minute/per-day limits
- Gemini: Regional and tier-based limits

Current workaround: Wait, or manually switch browser profiles. This kills productivity.

## What CAAM Does

CAAM is an OAuth token vault with instant profile switching:
- Stores encrypted OAuth tokens for multiple accounts per provider
- Detects rate limit responses automatically
- Switches to next available account in <100ms
- Tracks usage per account to predict limits
- Supports: Claude, OpenAI, Gemini, Anthropic API, etc.

### Key CAAM Commands
```bash
caam list                    # List all accounts
caam switch claude account2  # Switch to specific account
caam status                  # Show current account and usage
caam auto-rotate             # Enable automatic rotation on rate limit
```

## Integration Strategy for NTM

### 1. Rate Limit Detection
NTM monitors agent output for rate limit signals:
- "Rate limit exceeded"
- HTTP 429 responses
- Provider-specific messages

### 2. Automatic Recovery
When rate limit detected:
1. Pause agent activity
2. Call: caam switch <provider> --next
3. Update environment variables for agent
4. Resume agent

### 3. Robot Mode Commands
- --robot-switch-account: Manually trigger switch
- --robot-account-status: Get current account info
- --robot-accounts-list: List available accounts

### 4. Dashboard Integration
- Show current account per agent
- Show usage/limit percentages
- Alert when approaching 80% of limit

## Considerations
- Security: CAAM handles encryption, NTM just invokes CLI
- Atomicity: Account switch must be atomic (no half-switched state)
- Recovery: If CAAM fails, agent should continue with current account
- Latency: <100ms target for account switch","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-21T06:47:16.068201734Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T17:40:41.871111517Z","closed_at":"2026-01-21T17:40:41.871056724Z","close_reason":"All CAAM integration tasks complete: detection, config, robot commands, auto-switching, dashboard widget","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-8o977","depends_on_id":"bd-mk1i5","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:30Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-w254z","title":"AccountRotator: Detect current account state","description":"## Purpose
Detect when an agent hits usage limits by monitoring pane output and trigger account rotation through caam integration.

## Implementation Details

```go
// AccountRotator manages caam-based account switching on limit detection
type AccountRotator struct {
    caamPath       string           // Path to caam binary
    limitDetector  *LimitDetector
    rotationState  map[string]*RotationState // pane -> state
    mu             sync.RWMutex
    logger         *slog.Logger
}

type RotationState struct {
    CurrentAccount   string
    PreviousAccounts []string
    RotationCount    int
    LastRotation     time.Time
}

// OnLimitHit is called when LimitDetector detects a rate limit
func (ar *AccountRotator) OnLimitHit(event LimitHitEvent) error {
    ar.mu.Lock()
    defer ar.mu.Unlock()
    
    state := ar.getOrCreateState(event.SessionPane)
    
    ar.logger.Info("limit hit detected, initiating account rotation",
        "pane", event.SessionPane,
        "agent_type", event.AgentType,
        "pattern", event.Pattern,
        "current_account", state.CurrentAccount,
        "rotation_count", state.RotationCount)
    
    // Get next available account from caam
    newAccount, err := ar.getNextAccount(event.AgentType)
    if err != nil {
        ar.logger.Error("failed to get next account",
            "agent_type", event.AgentType,
            "error", err)
        return fmt.Errorf("get next account: %w", err)
    }
    
    // Track rotation
    state.PreviousAccounts = append(state.PreviousAccounts, state.CurrentAccount)
    state.CurrentAccount = newAccount
    state.RotationCount++
    state.LastRotation = time.Now()
    
    ar.logger.Info("account rotation prepared",
        "pane", event.SessionPane,
        "old_account", state.PreviousAccounts[len(state.PreviousAccounts)-1],
        "new_account", newAccount,
        "total_rotations", state.RotationCount)
    
    return nil
}
```

## Integration with caam
- Invoke `caam switch <provider> --next` to get next available account
- Parse caam JSON output for account details
- Handle case when no accounts are available

## Logging Requirements
- Log limit detection trigger with full event context
- Log current account before rotation
- Log new account after rotation
- Track total rotation count per pane
- Error logging with caam command details

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Detect limit hits from LimitDetector events
- [ ] Invoke caam CLI for account switching
- [ ] Track rotation history per pane
- [ ] Handle caam errors gracefully
- [ ] Prevent rapid rotation loops (cooldown)
- [ ] Structured logging at each rotation step","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-22T07:01:42.400024504Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T22:47:34.334499044Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T22:47:34.334476261Z","close_reason":"Implemented per-pane RotationState tracking, OnLimitHit handler with cooldown logic, and comprehensive tests","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-w254z","depends_on_id":"bd-3lo54","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-w254z","depends_on_id":"bd-3rngh","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}
 {"id":"bd-w56c","title":"Fix layout breakpoint transitions causing UI jumps","description":"## Problem
When terminal width crosses layout breakpoints (120, 200, 240 cols), UI suddenly reorganizes causing jarring visual jumps.

## Code Review Findings (2026-01-19)

### Confirmed Issue: No Hysteresis or Debounce

**dashboard.go:668-684** - Direct tier calculation on resize:
```go
func (m *Model) handleWindowSize(msg tea.WindowSizeMsg) (tea.Model, tea.Cmd) {
    m.width = width
    m.height = height
    m.tier = layout.TierForWidth(width)  // Immediate tier switch
}
```

**layout.go:54-67** - No hysteresis in tier detection - exact thresholds, no buffer

### Root Cause
- Tier changes happen instantly at exact pixel boundaries
- No debounce on resize events
- No smooth transition between layouts

## Fix Implementation

### 1. Add Hysteresis to Tier Detection
```go
// layout.go
const HysteresisBuffer = 5 // cols

func TierForWidthWithHysteresis(width int, currentTier Tier) Tier {
    newTier := TierForWidth(width)
    if newTier == currentTier {
        return currentTier
    }
    // Only switch if we've moved 5+ cols past threshold
    // ...implementation details...
}
```

### 2. Add Debounce to Resize Handler
Delay applying resize by 100ms to batch rapid events.

## Unit Tests Required
```go
func TestTierHysteresis(t *testing.T) {
    // Start at narrow, resize to just past threshold
    // At 120 exactly, should still be narrow (hysteresis)
    // At 125, should switch to split
    // At 118, should stay split (prevents switch back)
}
```

## E2E Test (VHS)
```tape
# layout_transition_test.tape
Set Width 250
Type "ntm dashboard --demo-data"
Sleep 2s
# Slowly resize down through breakpoints
Set Width 130
Sleep 1s
# Verify smooth transitions
```

## Verification
- [ ] Slowly resize through breakpoints - no sudden jumps
- [ ] Rapid resize - debounce prevents flickering
- [ ] Hysteresis prevents tier oscillation at exact thresholds","status":"closed","priority":2,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"ubuntu","created_at":"2026-01-19T07:10:30.167252379Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-19T09:17:35.076312014Z","closed_at":"2026-01-19T09:17:31.850130704Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-w56c","depends_on_id":"bd-36he","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-w56c","depends_on_id":"bd-3v69","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"}],"comments":[{"id":55,"issue_id":"bd-w56c","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Implemented hysteresis to prevent UI jumps at breakpoint boundaries:

1. Added HysteresisBuffer constant (5 cols) to layout.go
2. Added TierForWidthWithHysteresis() function that prevents tier switches until width passes threshold + buffer
3. Added thresholdForTier() helper function
4. Updated dashboard.go handleWindowSize() and WindowSizeMsg handler to use hysteresis
5. Created comprehensive test suite in layout_test.go with 40+ test cases

The fix prevents flickering when terminal width hovers near breakpoints (120, 200, 240, 320). All tests pass.","created_at":"2026-01-19T09:17:35Z"}]}
 {"id":"bd-whspn","title":"Task: Implement pre-compact context trigger","description":"## Overview
Implement the pre-compact trigger that detects when agents are approaching context limits and generates automatic handoffs BEFORE compaction occurs.

## Background & Reasoning
From Continuous-Claude-v3: The key insight is to capture context BEFORE compaction happens. Once compaction occurs, information is already lost. We need:
1. Context usage monitoring per agent
2. Threshold detection (70% = warn, 75%+ = auto-trigger)
3. Automatic handoff generation with agent context
4. Integration with dashboard for visibility

This is the core of "compound, don't compact" - we extract state and reload fresh instead of lossy compaction.

## Token Detection Mechanism

### Claude Code
- Read transcript size from `~/.claude/projects/{project}/session.jsonl`
- Estimate tokens: `file_size / 4` (rough approximation)
- Known limits: Claude = 200K tokens

### Codex CLI
- Parse context metrics from output (Codex reports usage)
- Monitor stderr for context warnings

### Gemini CLI
- Similar to Claude - monitor transcript/context file size

## Deliverables

### 1. Create internal/context/monitor.go
```go
package context

import (
    "log/slog"
    "os"
    "path/filepath"
    "sync"
    "time"
)

// Context limits by agent type (in tokens)
var DefaultLimits = map[string]int{
    "cc":  200000, // Claude 200K
    "cod": 128000, // Codex 128K
    "gmi": 1000000, // Gemini 1M
}

// Monitor tracks context window usage for agents
type Monitor struct {
    mu         sync.RWMutex
    agents     map[string]*AgentContext // key: paneID
    limits     map[string]int           // context limits by agent type
    logger     *slog.Logger
    projectDir string

    // Velocity tracking
    velocityWindow time.Duration
    history        map[string][]tokenSample
}

type tokenSample struct {
    tokens    int
    timestamp time.Time
}

type AgentContext struct {
    PaneID        string
    AgentType     string    // cc, cod, gmi
    SessionName   string
    CurrentTokens int
    MaxTokens     int
    LastUpdated   time.Time
    Velocity      float64   // tokens per minute (rolling average)
    TranscriptPath string   // path to agent's transcript file
}

// NewMonitor creates a context monitor with default limits
func NewMonitor(projectDir string) *Monitor {
    return &Monitor{
        agents:         make(map[string]*AgentContext),
        limits:         DefaultLimits,
        logger:         slog.Default().With("component", "context.monitor"),
        projectDir:     projectDir,
        velocityWindow: 5 * time.Minute,
        history:        make(map[string][]tokenSample),
    }
}

// RegisterAgent adds an agent to monitoring
func (m *Monitor) RegisterAgent(paneID, agentType, sessionName, transcriptPath string) {
    m.mu.Lock()
    defer m.mu.Unlock()

    maxTokens := m.limits[agentType]
    if maxTokens == 0 {
        maxTokens = 100000 // Default fallback
    }

    m.agents[paneID] = &AgentContext{
        PaneID:         paneID,
        AgentType:      agentType,
        SessionName:    sessionName,
        MaxTokens:      maxTokens,
        TranscriptPath: transcriptPath,
    }

    m.logger.Info("registered agent for context monitoring",
        "pane_id", paneID,
        "agent_type", agentType,
        "session", sessionName,
        "max_tokens", maxTokens,
    )
}

// Update records new context information for an agent
func (m *Monitor) Update(paneID string, tokens int) error {
    m.mu.Lock()
    defer m.mu.Unlock()

    agent, ok := m.agents[paneID]
    if !ok {
        return fmt.Errorf("agent not registered: %s", paneID)
    }

    oldTokens := agent.CurrentTokens
    agent.CurrentTokens = tokens
    agent.LastUpdated = time.Now()

    // Update velocity history
    m.history[paneID] = append(m.history[paneID], tokenSample{
        tokens:    tokens,
        timestamp: time.Now(),
    })
    // Trim old samples
    m.trimHistory(paneID)

    // Calculate velocity
    agent.Velocity = m.calculateVelocity(paneID)

    m.logger.Debug("updated context",
        "pane_id", paneID,
        "tokens", tokens,
        "delta", tokens-oldTokens,
        "velocity_tpm", agent.Velocity,
        "usage_pct", float64(tokens)/float64(agent.MaxTokens)*100,
    )

    return nil
}

// UpdateFromTranscript reads the transcript file and estimates tokens
func (m *Monitor) UpdateFromTranscript(paneID string) error {
    m.mu.RLock()
    agent, ok := m.agents[paneID]
    m.mu.RUnlock()

    if !ok {
        return fmt.Errorf("agent not registered: %s", paneID)
    }

    if agent.TranscriptPath == "" {
        return nil // No transcript to monitor
    }

    info, err := os.Stat(agent.TranscriptPath)
    if err != nil {
        if os.IsNotExist(err) {
            return nil // File doesn't exist yet
        }
        return err
    }

    // Estimate tokens from file size (rough: 4 bytes per token)
    estimatedTokens := int(info.Size() / 4)

    return m.Update(paneID, estimatedTokens)
}

// GetUsage returns context usage percentage for an agent
func (m *Monitor) GetUsage(paneID string) (float64, error) {
    m.mu.RLock()
    defer m.mu.RUnlock()

    agent, ok := m.agents[paneID]
    if !ok {
        return 0, fmt.Errorf("agent not registered: %s", paneID)
    }

    if agent.MaxTokens == 0 {
        return 0, nil
    }

    return float64(agent.CurrentTokens) / float64(agent.MaxTokens) * 100, nil
}

// GetAllUsage returns usage for all agents
func (m *Monitor) GetAllUsage() map[string]float64 {
    m.mu.RLock()
    defer m.mu.RUnlock()

    result := make(map[string]float64)
    for paneID, agent := range m.agents {
        if agent.MaxTokens > 0 {
            result[paneID] = float64(agent.CurrentTokens) / float64(agent.MaxTokens) * 100
        }
    }
    return result
}

// ShouldTriggerHandoff returns true if agent should create handoff
// Returns (should_trigger, reason)
func (m *Monitor) ShouldTriggerHandoff(paneID string) (bool, string) {
    m.mu.RLock()
    defer m.mu.RUnlock()

    agent, ok := m.agents[paneID]
    if !ok {
        return false, ""
    }

    usage := float64(agent.CurrentTokens) / float64(agent.MaxTokens) * 100

    // Hard threshold
    if usage >= 75 {
        return true, fmt.Sprintf("usage %.1f%% exceeds 75%% threshold", usage)
    }

    // Velocity-based prediction: if we'll hit 85% in 2 minutes
    if agent.Velocity > 0 {
        tokensIn2Min := agent.Velocity * 2
        predictedUsage := float64(agent.CurrentTokens+int(tokensIn2Min)) / float64(agent.MaxTokens) * 100
        if predictedUsage >= 85 {
            return true, fmt.Sprintf("velocity %.0f tpm predicts %.1f%% in 2 min", agent.Velocity, predictedUsage)
        }
    }

    return false, ""
}

// GetAgent returns agent context (for handoff metadata)
func (m *Monitor) GetAgent(paneID string) (*AgentContext, bool) {
    m.mu.RLock()
    defer m.mu.RUnlock()
    agent, ok := m.agents[paneID]
    if !ok {
        return nil, false
    }
    // Return copy to avoid race
    copy := *agent
    return &copy, true
}

func (m *Monitor) trimHistory(paneID string) {
    cutoff := time.Now().Add(-m.velocityWindow)
    history := m.history[paneID]
    var trimmed []tokenSample
    for _, s := range history {
        if s.timestamp.After(cutoff) {
            trimmed = append(trimmed, s)
        }
    }
    m.history[paneID] = trimmed
}

func (m *Monitor) calculateVelocity(paneID string) float64 {
    history := m.history[paneID]
    if len(history) < 2 {
        return 0
    }

    first := history[0]
    last := history[len(history)-1]
    duration := last.timestamp.Sub(first.timestamp).Minutes()
    if duration < 0.5 {
        return 0 // Not enough time span
    }

    tokenDelta := last.tokens - first.tokens
    if tokenDelta < 0 {
        return 0 // Tokens decreased (session reset?)
    }

    return float64(tokenDelta) / duration
}
```

### 2. Create internal/context/trigger.go
```go
package context

import (
    "log/slog"
    "sync"
    "time"

    "github.com/yourusername/ntm/internal/handoff"
)

// Trigger handles automatic handoff generation
type Trigger struct {
    monitor       *Monitor
    generator     *handoff.Generator
    writer        *handoff.Writer
    reader        *handoff.Reader
    logger        *slog.Logger

    mu            sync.Mutex
    lastTrigger   map[string]time.Time // paneID -> last trigger time
    cooldown      time.Duration        // minimum time between triggers per agent

    // Callbacks
    onTrigger     func(paneID, path string) // called when handoff generated
    onWarning     func(paneID string, usage float64) // called at warning threshold
}

// NewTrigger creates a pre-compact trigger
func NewTrigger(projectDir string, monitor *Monitor) *Trigger {
    return &Trigger{
        monitor:     monitor,
        generator:   handoff.NewGenerator(projectDir),
        writer:      handoff.NewWriter(projectDir),
        reader:      handoff.NewReader(projectDir),
        logger:      slog.Default().With("component", "context.trigger"),
        lastTrigger: make(map[string]time.Time),
        cooldown:    5 * time.Minute,
    }
}

// SetCallbacks registers callbacks for trigger events
func (t *Trigger) SetCallbacks(onTrigger func(string, string), onWarning func(string, float64)) {
    t.onTrigger = onTrigger
    t.onWarning = onWarning
}

// Check evaluates all agents and triggers handoffs as needed
// Returns list of generated handoff paths
func (t *Trigger) Check() ([]string, error) {
    t.logger.Debug("checking all agents for context triggers")

    var paths []string

    usage := t.monitor.GetAllUsage()
    for paneID, pct := range usage {
        // Warning callback at 70%
        if pct >= 70 && pct < 75 && t.onWarning != nil {
            t.onWarning(paneID, pct)
        }

        // Check if should trigger
        shouldTrigger, reason := t.monitor.ShouldTriggerHandoff(paneID)
        if !shouldTrigger {
            continue
        }

        // Check cooldown
        t.mu.Lock()
        lastTime := t.lastTrigger[paneID]
        if time.Since(lastTime) < t.cooldown {
            t.mu.Unlock()
            t.logger.Debug("skipping trigger due to cooldown",
                "pane_id", paneID,
                "last_trigger", lastTime,
                "cooldown", t.cooldown,
            )
            continue
        }
        t.lastTrigger[paneID] = time.Now()
        t.mu.Unlock()

        path, err := t.TriggerForAgent(paneID)
        if err != nil {
            t.logger.Error("failed to trigger handoff",
                "pane_id", paneID,
                "error", err,
            )
            continue
        }

        t.logger.Info("auto-handoff triggered",
            "pane_id", paneID,
            "reason", reason,
            "path", path,
        )

        paths = append(paths, path)

        // Callback
        if t.onTrigger != nil {
            t.onTrigger(paneID, path)
        }
    }

    return paths, nil
}

// TriggerForAgent forces handoff generation for specific agent
func (t *Trigger) TriggerForAgent(paneID string) (string, error) {
    agent, ok := t.monitor.GetAgent(paneID)
    if !ok {
        return "", fmt.Errorf("agent not found: %s", paneID)
    }

    t.logger.Debug("triggering handoff for agent",
        "pane_id", paneID,
        "session", agent.SessionName,
        "usage_pct", float64(agent.CurrentTokens)/float64(agent.MaxTokens)*100,
    )

    // Generate handoff from transcript if available
    var h *handoff.Handoff
    var err error

    if agent.TranscriptPath != "" {
        h, err = t.generator.GenerateFromTranscript(agent.SessionName, agent.TranscriptPath)
    } else {
        // Create minimal handoff with context info
        h = &handoff.Handoff{
            Session: agent.SessionName,
            Goal:    "Auto-handoff due to context limit",
            Now:     "Review and continue previous work",
            Status:  handoff.StatusPartial,
        }
    }

    if err != nil {
        t.logger.Warn("transcript generation failed, using minimal handoff",
            "pane_id", paneID,
            "error", err,
        )
        h = &handoff.Handoff{
            Session: agent.SessionName,
            Goal:    "Auto-handoff due to context limit",
            Now:     "Review and continue previous work",
            Status:  handoff.StatusPartial,
        }
    }

    // Add context metadata
    h.AgentID = paneID
    h.AgentType = agent.AgentType
    h.PaneID = paneID
    h.TokensUsed = agent.CurrentTokens
    h.TokensMax = agent.MaxTokens
    h.TokensPct = float64(agent.CurrentTokens) / float64(agent.MaxTokens) * 100

    // Enrich with git state
    t.generator.EnrichWithGitState(h)

    // Write auto-handoff
    path, err := t.writer.WriteAuto(h)
    if err != nil {
        return "", fmt.Errorf("write auto-handoff: %w", err)
    }

    // Invalidate reader cache
    t.reader.InvalidateCache()

    return path, nil
}

// StartPeriodicCheck starts a goroutine that checks at intervals
func (t *Trigger) StartPeriodicCheck(interval time.Duration, stop <-chan struct{}) {
    t.logger.Info("starting periodic context check",
        "interval", interval,
    )

    ticker := time.NewTicker(interval)
    defer ticker.Stop()

    for {
        select {
        case <-ticker.C:
            if paths, err := t.Check(); err != nil {
                t.logger.Error("periodic check failed", "error", err)
            } else if len(paths) > 0 {
                t.logger.Info("periodic check generated handoffs",
                    "count", len(paths),
                )
            }
        case <-stop:
            t.logger.Info("stopping periodic context check")
            return
        }
    }
}
```

### 3. Threshold Configuration
```yaml
# In ntm.yaml
context_rotation:
  enabled: true
  warn_threshold: 70      # Show warning in dashboard
  trigger_threshold: 75   # Generate auto-handoff
  check_interval: 30s     # How often to check
  cooldown: 5m            # Minimum time between triggers per agent
  velocity_weight: 0.3    # Factor in token velocity prediction
```

### 4. Dashboard Integration Points

**Status Panel:**
- Show context % per agent in status
- Color coding: green (<50%), yellow (50-70%), orange (70-85%), red (85%+)
- Velocity indicator: ↑ if increasing rapidly

**Alerts:**
- Warning alert at 70%: "Agent cc_1 context at 72%"
- Trigger alert at 75%+: "Auto-handoff generated for cc_1"

**Callbacks:**
- `onWarning(paneID, usage)` - dashboard can show warning
- `onTrigger(paneID, path)` - dashboard can show notification

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] Monitor tracks token usage per agent
- [ ] Monitor registers agents with type, session, transcript path
- [ ] Update accepts token counts and updates velocity
- [ ] UpdateFromTranscript estimates tokens from file size
- [ ] Velocity calculated from rolling 5-minute window
- [ ] ShouldTriggerHandoff checks 75% threshold
- [ ] ShouldTriggerHandoff considers velocity prediction
- [ ] Trigger respects cooldown between triggers
- [ ] TriggerForAgent generates handoff with context metadata
- [ ] Check iterates all agents and returns generated paths
- [ ] Warning callback called at 70%
- [ ] Trigger callback called when handoff generated
- [ ] StartPeriodicCheck runs at configured interval
- [ ] All operations logged with structured slog
- [ ] Unit tests for monitor with various token scenarios
- [ ] Unit tests for velocity calculation
- [ ] Unit tests for trigger thresholds and cooldown
- [ ] Integration test with mock agents

## Technical Notes
- Token estimation from file size: ~4 bytes per token (rough)
- Velocity in tokens per minute, rolling 5-minute window
- Cooldown prevents rapid-fire handoffs
- Callbacks allow dashboard integration without tight coupling
- Reader cache invalidated after trigger to ensure fresh reads

## Logging Requirements
- DEBUG: Update with tokens, delta, velocity, usage%
- DEBUG: Check start, cooldown skips
- DEBUG: TriggerForAgent start with context info
- INFO: Agent registered for monitoring
- INFO: Auto-handoff triggered with reason and path
- INFO: Periodic check started/stopped
- WARN: Transcript generation failures
- ERROR: Check failures, write failures","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","created_at":"2026-01-19T18:49:57.637574879Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-20T18:35:28.171248607Z","closed_at":"2026-01-20T18:35:28.171126106Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"bd-whspn","depends_on_id":"bd-2f6wa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-whspn","depends_on_id":"bd-j44fa","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"},{"issue_id":"bd-whspn","depends_on_id":"bd-khux0","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-30T23:34:31Z","created_by":"import"}]}

 {"id":"bd-wx2qv","title":"Feature: Mode Catalog & Data Model - Define reasoning mode types and storage","description":"# Feature: Mode Catalog & Data Model (Core Types)

**Internal infrastructure** — users interact via ensembles; modes are implementation detail.

## Core Types

### ReasoningMode
```go
type ReasoningMode struct {
    ID            string   // e.g., "deductive"
    Code          string   // e.g., "A1" (taxonomy code)
    Tier          ModeTier // core|advanced|experimental
    Category      ModeCategory
    Name          string
    ShortDesc     string
    Description   string
    Outputs       string
    BestFor       []string
    FailureModes  []string
    Differentiator string
    Icon          string
    Color         string
    PreambleKey   string
}
```

### Ensemble (Primary UX)
```go
type EnsembleConfig struct {
    Name        string
    Display     string
    Description string
    Modes       []ModeRef
    Synthesis   SynthesisConfig
    Budget      BudgetConfig
    AllowAdvanced bool
}
```

### OutputSchema (Mandatory)
```go
type ModeOutput struct {
    Thesis           string
    TopFindings      []Finding
    Risks            []Risk
    Recommendations  []Recommendation
    QuestionsForUser []Question
    FailureModesToWatch []FailureModeWarning
}
```

## Key Decisions
1. **Full 80-mode catalog ships** (bd-11mtt) with tiered filtering
2. **Modes are internal** — ensembles are the user-facing unit
3. **Schema is mandatory** — every output must conform to ModeOutput
4. **No file sprawl** — keep types centralized in `internal/ensemble/types.go`

## File Structure
- internal/ensemble/types.go (core types + validation)
- internal/ensemble/modes.go (embedded 80-mode catalog)
- internal/ensemble/ensembles.go (built-in ensembles)
- internal/ensemble/schema_validator.go (schema validation)

## Status
Closed after core types/tests implemented; catalog population and preambles tracked in bd-11mtt + bd-2pl9v.","status":"closed","priority":1,"issue_type":"feature","created_at":"2026-01-23T07:25:38.907035393Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-23T18:01:23.990452770Z","closed_at":"2026-01-23T08:13:55.265977332Z","close_reason":"Implemented output schema types (ImpactLevel, Confidence, Finding, Risk, Recommendation, Question, FailureModeWarning, ModeOutput), configuration types (BudgetConfig, SynthesisConfig, Ensemble), and comprehensive unit tests.
